page: Amazon Web Services r6a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 virtual CPUs across 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz and includes a 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate a balanced performance profile. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core CPU performance falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average read performance for both small and large blocks, but large block memory write bandwidth is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%. Standard operations such as Redis SET, static web serving, and database operations consistently score in the average tier.

The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs limits this instance's utility for high-speed local caching or hardware-accelerated machine learning. For instance, LLM inference benchmarks show weak performance for medium-sized models and poor performance for large models. However, the high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation make it suitable for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications that do not require local disk storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 8 physical cores and 2 threads per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, while multi-core CPU, memory read bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Conversely, large block memory write bandwidth is weak. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, and caching layers that require high memory-to-CPU ratios without the need for local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory density, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and balanced single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB DDR4 RAM providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.6 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-core CPU performance and average database operation speeds
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
